label: White House
sub: Center of Trump executive power. Site of hundreds of executive orders, press briefings, and key policy meetings across both terms.

label: US Capitol
sub: Site of Trump's two impeachments, both inaugurations, and the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack by supporters seeking to halt election certification.

label: US Supreme Court
sub: Trump appointed three justices (Gorsuch, Kavanaugh, Barrett), creating a 6-3 conservative majority. Key rulings include upholding Travel Ban, reversing Roe v. Wade, and granting presidential immunity.

label: Mar-a-Lago, Palm Beach FL
sub: Trump's private club and primary residence, used as the 'Winter White House' during both terms. Site of the August 2022 FBI search for classified documents and frequent foreign leader meetings.

label: Trump Tower, New York City
sub: Trump campaign headquarters in 2016 and symbolic HQ of the Trump Organization. Site of the 2016 Trump Tower meeting with Russians. Subject to NY AG and Manhattan DA investigations.

label: Manhattan Criminal Courthouse, NYC
sub: Site of Trump's 34-count felony hush money trial (2024). Trump became the first former US president convicted of felony crimes here on May 30, 2024.

label: Fulton County Courthouse, Atlanta GA
sub: Site of the Georgia RICO indictment (August 2023) of Trump and 18 co-defendants for alleged conspiracy to overturn Georgia's 2020 election results, including the recorded Raffensperger call.

label: Federal Courthouse, Miami FL
sub: Site of Trump's federal classified documents trial before Judge Aileen Cannon. The case (37 federal counts) was ultimately dismissed by Cannon in July 2024 citing concerns about the Special Counsel's appointment.

label: US District Court, Washington DC
sub: Site of Trump's federal January 6 indictment (4 counts including conspiracy). Special Counsel Jack Smith dropped the case in November 2024 per DOJ policy against indicting a sitting president-elect.

label: The Ellipse, Washington DC
sub: Site of the 'Save America' rally on January 6, 2021, where Trump gave a 70-minute speech urging supporters to march to the Capitol and 'fight like hell.' A key location in the incitement of insurrection case.

label: Pentagon, Arlington VA
sub: HQ of the Department of Defense. Multiple defense secretaries resigned or were fired under Trump. Site of controversies over military use at the US-Mexico border and Lafayette Square.

label: Eagle Pass, TX — Border Crossing
sub: One of the busiest and most contested US-Mexico border crossing points during Trump's immigration crackdowns. Texas Governor Abbott sent National Guard and placed buoys in the Rio Grande here; federal-state border confrontations became a major story.

label: El Paso, TX
sub: Major border city and key immigration processing hub. Site of the August 3, 2019 mass shooting at a Walmart targeting Hispanics, and overcrowded border processing facilities during surge years.

label: Laredo, TX — Border Port of Entry
sub: One of the largest US-Mexico land ports, processing billions in trade. Center of Trump administration's border enforcement efforts and subject to trade disruption from tariffs on Mexican imports.

label: Nogales, AZ — Border Crossing
sub: Key US-Mexico border port in Arizona. Site of significant border wall construction during Trump's first term and repeated large-scale migrant encounters. Connected to Trump's 'zero tolerance' family separation implementation.

label: McAllen, TX — Rio Grande Valley
sub: Rio Grande Valley border sector, consistently the highest-traffic corridor for illegal crossings during 2018-2023 surges. Site where family separation controversy was most acute in 2018.

label: Yuma, AZ — Border Sector
sub: Arizona border sector that saw dramatic migrant surge in 2021-2022. Trump focused on completing border wall sections in this area during his first term. Site of multiple 'field trip' visits by politicians.

label: San Diego — Tijuana Border
sub: Major urban border crossing and processing center. Site of 2018 migrant caravan confrontations and tear gas use on asylum seekers. One of the most fenced and monitored sections of the US-Mexico border.

label: Pittsburgh, PA — Steel Industry Hub
sub: Symbolic center of the US steel industry that Trump sought to protect with 25% steel tariffs (2018, 2025). Pennsylvania steel workers were key Trump political constituents; tariffs temporarily boosted domestic steel production.

label: Detroit, MI — Auto Industry
sub: Center of US auto manufacturing, heavily impacted by Trump tariff policies on steel, aluminum, and auto parts. Trump repeatedly targeted USMCA and auto trade with Canada and Mexico; the 2025 auto tariff threats destabilized supply chains.

label: Ames, IA — Farm Belt / Iowa Caucuses
sub: Iowa, a key Trump political base and major agricultural state, was severely impacted by China retaliatory tariffs on soybeans and corn. The administration created $28 billion in farm aid payments over 2018-2019 to offset trade war losses. Iowa also hosted the first Republican primary caucuses.

label: Port of Los Angeles, CA
sub: The largest US seaport by container volume, handling the majority of US-China trade. Trade disruptions from escalating tariffs caused significant shipping volume declines and supply chain disruptions throughout Trump's tariff campaigns.

label: Port of Seattle / Boeing HQ Area
sub: Home of Boeing, a major US exporter targeted by Chinese and EU retaliatory tariffs. Aerospace exports are among the most impacted by trade wars; the EU-US Airbus-Boeing dispute intensified under Trump.

label: Butler, PA — Assassination Attempt Site
sub: Site of the July 13, 2024 assassination attempt on Trump during a campaign rally. A gunman struck Trump's right ear, killed one attendee, and critically wounded two others before being shot by Secret Service. The iconic fist-raise photograph became an emblem of the 2024 campaign.

label: West Palm Beach, FL — Trump Golf Course
sub: Site of the September 15, 2024 second assassination attempt, where a Secret Service agent spotted a rifle-armed man in the bushes near Trump's golf course. The suspect was apprehended; Trump was not harmed.

label: Charlottesville, VA — Unite the Right Rally
sub: Site of the August 12, 2017 white supremacist rally that killed counter-protester Heather Heyer. Trump's 'very fine people on both sides' comment became one of the most controversial statements of his first term.

label: Lafayette Square, Washington DC
sub: On June 1, 2020, law enforcement using tear gas and batons cleared peaceful BLM protesters from Lafayette Square so Trump could walk to St. John's Church for a Bible photo op. The incident caused senior military and security officials to publicly distance themselves from Trump.

label: Minneapolis, MN — George Floyd Square
sub: Site of George Floyd's murder by police on May 25, 2020, sparking nationwide BLM protests. The incident prompted national debate over police reform and Trump's response — threatening to invoke the Insurrection Act — drew bipartisan criticism.

label: Kenosha, WI — BLM Protests / Kyle Rittenhouse
sub: Site of major BLM protests following the police shooting of Jacob Blake (August 23, 2020). Trump made multiple statements praising Kyle Rittenhouse, who shot three protesters. Trump visited Kenosha; Democrats said the visit inflamed tensions.

label: Walter Reed Medical Center, Bethesda MD
sub: Trump was hospitalized here for three days after contracting COVID-19 in October 2020. He received experimental monoclonal antibody treatment and steroids. His dramatic balcony return — removing his mask — generated widespread controversy.

label: Trump National Golf Club, Bedminster NJ
sub: Trump's New Jersey golf property, frequently used as a 'Summer White House.' Site of major fundraisers, foreign leader meetings, and LIV Golf events. Trump spent significant time here during both terms.

label: Iowa — 2024 Primary Caucus
sub: Trump won the January 2024 Iowa Republican caucuses with 51% of the vote — the highest share in Iowa caucus history — effectively ending the primary contest. Iowa was also a key swing state Trump won in 2016 and 2020.

label: New Hampshire — 2024 Republican Primary
sub: Trump won the New Hampshire Republican primary in January 2024 against Nikki Haley, his last remaining major primary challenger. Despite Haley's relative success (41%), Trump's dominance effectively secured the nomination.

label: Milwaukee, WI — 2024 Republican National Convention
sub: The 2024 Republican National Convention was held in Milwaukee July 15-18. Trump appeared with his ear bandaged from the assassination attempt, accepted the nomination, and selected JD Vance as running mate. The convention cemented the MAGA movement's complete dominance of the Republican Party.

label: Dover Air Force Base, DE
sub: Site of dignified transfer ceremonies for US military fallen. Trump attended multiple ceremonies here. The contrast between his respectful presence and his reported 2020 comments calling fallen soldiers 'suckers and losers' (denied by Trump) became a major controversy.

label: Harley-Davidson HQ, Milwaukee WI
sub: Harley-Davidson was among the first US companies to announce production moves abroad (to Thailand) in response to EU retaliatory tariffs on motorcycles (in response to Trump's steel tariffs). Trump threatened to tax Harley-Davidson 'like never before' for moving jobs out.

label: Boeing Plant, North Charleston SC
sub: Major US aerospace manufacturing site. Boeing's 787 Dreamliner production here benefited from Trump's 'Buy American' rhetoric but faced headwinds from tariff-driven supply chain disruptions and China's retaliatory tariffs on US aircraft.

label: Foxconn Site, Mount Pleasant WI
sub: Trump personally promoted Foxconn's promised $10 billion factory that would create 13,000 jobs in Wisconsin. The deal received massive state tax subsidies but Foxconn scaled back dramatically and ultimately built a far smaller facility, becoming a symbol of unmet manufacturing promises.

label: El Salvador CECOT Prison (via US Deal)
sub: Trump struck a deal with El Salvador's President Bukele to house Venezuelan deportees at CECOT, the mega-prison complex. Hundreds of Venezuelans deported under the Alien Enemies Act were sent here in March 2025, triggering legal battles over due process and human rights.

label: Arizona/Sonora Border — Wall Construction
sub: Central location for border wall construction activity during Trump's first term. Over 450 miles of new or replacement barrier was built, primarily in Arizona, California, and New Mexico. Trump declared the wall built; critics noted much was replacement, not new.

label: US Treasury Department, Washington DC
sub: Headquarters for tariff and sanctions policy under both Trump terms. Treasury Secretaries Steve Mnuchin (1st term) and Scott Bessent (2nd term) managed trade negotiations with China, Canada, Mexico, and others amid tariff escalations.
